prompt_md: A product catalog must record that each product belongs to one or more categories, and each category contains many products. The team currently stores a single `category_id` foreign key on the Product row, but products clearly need to appear in several categories at once. What is the best way to model this relationship?

decision_choices: Add more category_id columns to Product (category_id_2, category_id_3, ...)
Introduce a junction table holding (product_id, category_id) pairs
Store a comma-separated list of category IDs in one Product column
Duplicate the product as a separate row for each category
reason_choices: Because a many-to-many relationship needs its own table of key pairs, which scales to any number of links without duplication
Because adding fixed extra columns is the most flexible way to grow relationships
Because a delimited list lets the database enforce each category reference
Because duplicating the product keeps every category fully independent
